validity.warm_up_handling: manifest warm-up policy 'discard-requests' (50 requests per repetition, ordered by scheduled_send_ts): 150 events excluded, 2550 kept (ib-t010-e2b-overload-r1/rep1: 50 excluded; ib-t010-e2b-overload-r2/rep2: 50 excluded; ib-t010-e2b-overload-r3/rep3: 50 excluded)
threats_to_validity: deliberate 5x-capacity overload point (E2's own workload file reused verbatim): the elevated typed shed rate IS part of the expected trade at this shallower cap=1 queue (higher than E2's cap=3 5x shed rate of 77.53%, as this hypothesis predicted), not a failure mode being hidden; the declared gate threshold is raised to 0.95 for exactly this point, disclosed here, and the latency table below covers ACCEPTED requests only (shed requests never produce a first byte)
no rate sweep in this run set — knee_estimate is null; no claim is made about saturation behavior
no cost profile applies to this run set — cost is null (cost figures are only computed from a declared, provenanced cost-profile file, never from assumed rates)
